facility_name: Meta (Facebook) Odense Data Center -- 45MW Waste Heat to 7,000 Homes via Fjernvarme Fyn
build_phase_if_known: Operational since 2020. 50,000 sqm campus. 176 cooling units on roof.
cooling_type: Water-cooled heat recovery system via copper coils in 176 rooftop cooling units; warm water circulated to Fjernvarme Fyn heat pump plant
opportunity_summary: Meta's Odense data center campus (50,000 sqm) recovers waste heat via a water-based system. District heating water circulates through insulated steel pipes into rooftop copper coils across 176 cooling units, picking up low-temperature heat, then returns to Fjernvarme Fyn's 45MW heat pump plant -- Denmark's largest at commissioning. Delivers approximately 125,000 MWh of district heating annually to nearly 7,000 households in Odense. Ramboll is engineering partner. Supports Fjernvarme Fyn's 100% renewable energy goal by 2030.
confidence_reason: Extensively documented by Meta sustainability reports, Ramboll project page, Fjernvarme Fyn, DCD, State of Green Denmark, and EU Covenant of Mayors. Heat pump capacity (45MW) and homes heated (~7,000) confirmed by multiple independent sources.
source_1_title: Meta: surplus heat to district heating
source_1_evidence_snippet: Fjernvarme Fyn commissioned the world's largest district heating centre based on surplus heat from a data centre. Heat pump installation of approximately 45MW going directly into the district heating network in Odense.
source_2_title: Facebook Sustainability Denmark data center to warm local community
source_2_evidence_snippet: Facebook's data center in Odense produces approximately 125,000 MWh of district heating to almost 7,000 households.
notes: HEAT RECOVERY. World's largest DC heat pump plant at commissioning (45MW). 176 rooftop cooling units with copper coils. Warm water circulated via insulated steel pipes. Ramboll engineering partner. Fjernvarme Fyn is Denmark's third largest district heating company. 125,000 MWh/year to ~7,000 homes. Liquid cooling CONFIRMED by water circulation system.
heat_recovery_details: 45MW heat pump plant by Fjernvarme Fyn; 125,000 MWh/year district heating; ~7,000 homes heated in Odense; warm water from 176 rooftop cooling units piped to heat pump facility
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Fjernvarme Fyn commissioned the world's largest district heating centre based on surplus heat from a data centre in Odense.

facility_name: Microsoft / Fortum -- Espoo & Kirkkonummi Data Centers (World's Largest DC Heat Recovery, 40% District Heat)
build_phase_if_known: Heat pump plant construction started Jan 2025. Heat production expected 2025-2026 heating season. Fortum investing EUR 225M in waste heat projects.
cooling_type: Liquid cooling with heat pump recovery by Fortum; air-to-water heat pumps and electrical boilers; waste heat transferred to district heating network
opportunity_summary: Described as the world's largest data center heat recovery project and Finland's largest single ICT investment. Microsoft's two new data centers in Espoo and Kirkkonummi will provide approximately 40% of all district heat needed for 250,000 customers. Fortum invests EUR 225 million in heat pump plants. 75% of waste heat used annually. Saves 400,000 tonnes CO2/year at full capacity. Heat pump plant construction started Jan 2025, noisy construction ending June 2026. AFRY is technical consultant.
confidence_reason: Announced jointly by Microsoft and Fortum with full press releases. Confirmed by Fortum investor communications (EUR 225M investment), City of Espoo press release, AFRY project page, DCD, and Bloomberg. Construction photos and permits publicly visible.
source_1_title: Fortum and Microsoft's datacentre project spearheads energy efficiency
source_1_evidence_snippet: Microsoft's two new datacenters will eventually provide approximately 40% of all the district heat needed in the area. About 75% of waste heat used annually.
source_2_title: Microsoft announces intent to build a new datacenter region in Finland
source_2_evidence_snippet: World's largest collaboration to heat homes, services and businesses with sustainable waste heat from new data centre region. 250,000 district heat users served.
notes: HEAT RECOVERY. World's largest DC waste heat recovery project. Fortum EUR 225M investment in heat pump plants at both sites. 400,000 tonnes CO2 reduction/year at full capacity. 100% emission-free electricity. AFRY technical consultant. Construction started Jan 10, 2025 at Kirkkonummi. Heat production expected 2025-2026 heating season. Liquid cooling CONFIRMED -- heat recovery requires liquid medium.
heat_recovery_details: 40% of district heat for 250,000 customers; Fortum heat pumps + electrical boilers; 75% annual waste heat utilization; 400,000 tonnes CO2 savings/year; EUR 225M Fortum investment
current_phase: Heat pump plant construction at Kirkkonummi; data center construction at both sites
expansion_details: Full buildout will supply 40% of Espoo/Kauniainen/Kirkkonummi district heating demand
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Construction of Fortum's heat pump plant has started at Microsoft's data centre site in Kirkkonummi. Expected to produce district heating in 2025-2026 heating season.

facility_name: Google Hamina Data Center -- Waste Heat Covers 80% of City District Heating
build_phase_if_known: Operational since 2011. EUR 1 billion total investment. Heat recovery system operational end of 2025 via Nohewa 7.5MW heat pump plant.
cooling_type: Seawater cooling (Gulf of Finland); warm cooling water routed to Nohewa 7.5MW heat pump plant for district heating
opportunity_summary: Google's first offsite heat recovery project. Warm cooling water from the data center is supplied to a 7.5MW Nohewa heat pump plant that produces 40GWh of district heating per year, covering 80% of Hamina's total district heating needs (~2,000 households). Heat is 97% carbon free. Provided free of charge to Haminan Energia. EUR 1 billion total investment in facility. Located in converted paper mill.
confidence_reason: Google official blog announcement, Nohewa press release, Haminan Energia confirmation, DCD, Energy Digital, and Sustainability Magazine all corroborate. 7.5MW heat pump capacity and 80% coverage figure confirmed by multiple sources.
source_1_title: Our first offsite heat recovery project lands in Finland
source_1_evidence_snippet: Heat from the data center will provide 80 percent of the network's requirements. 97% carbon free recovered heat provided free of charge to Haminan Energia.
source_2_title: Heat recovery for Haminan Energia and Google data center in Hamina
source_2_evidence_snippet: 7.5MW heat pump plant can produce 40GWh of district heating per year. Corresponds to heating needs of approximately 2000 households.
notes: HEAT RECOVERY. Google's first offsite heat recovery project globally. Located in converted Stora Enso paper mill. Seawater cooling from Gulf of Finland. Nohewa heat pump plant (7.5MW). 40GWh/year = 80% of Hamina district heating. 97% carbon free. ~2,000 tonnes CO2 reduction/year. Heat provided FREE to Haminan Energia. Liquid cooling CONFIRMED by water-based heat transfer.
heat_recovery_details: 7.5MW Nohewa heat pump plant; 40GWh/year; 80% of Hamina district heating demand; ~2,000 households; 97% carbon-free heat; provided free to Haminan Energia
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Starting in 2025, warmth coming from the data centre will be recovered to optimise the district heating network energy efficiency.

facility_name: AWS Tallaght Data Center -- Ireland's First District Heating Scheme (100% DC Heat)
build_phase_if_known: District heating scheme operational. 704 metric tons CO2 abated in 2024. Heatworks utility operational.
cooling_type: Liquid-based heat recovery system; waste heat transferred via heat pumps to district heating network operated by Heatworks
opportunity_summary: Ireland's first large-scale district heating network. AWS provides 100% of heat to the Tallaght District Heating Scheme free of charge. Heats 47,000 sqm of public buildings (County Hall, library, Innovation Centre), 3,000 sqm commercial space, and 135 affordable rental apartments. Heatworks is Ireland's first not-for-profit energy utility (formed 2020 by South Dublin County Council). 704 metric tons CO2 abated in 2024. 60% carbon reduction vs conventional heating.
confidence_reason: Confirmed by About Amazon EU press release, South Dublin County Council official page, SEAI (Sustainable Energy Authority of Ireland) case study, CNBC feature, RTE news, and Irish Times. 100% heat supply and Heatworks utility confirmed across multiple independent sources.
source_1_title: Local community buildings in Ireland to be heated by Amazon data centre
source_1_evidence_snippet: AWS will provide recycled heat free of charge to the scheme. Heatworks utility sells heat at low cost to end users.
source_2_title: Tallaght District Heating Network And Energy Centre Officially Opened
source_2_evidence_snippet: Waste heat from the nearby AWS data centre supplies 100% of the heat to the network. 704 metric tons of CO2 abated in 2024.
notes: HEAT RECOVERY. Ireland's first large-scale district heating. 100% heat from AWS DC. Heatworks = Ireland's first not-for-profit energy utility. 135 affordable apartments + public buildings. Heat provided FREE by AWS. 704t CO2 abated in 2024. Liquid cooling CONFIRMED by heat recovery water system.
heat_recovery_details: 100% of district heating from AWS DC; 47,000 sqm public buildings + 135 apartments; 704t CO2 abated in 2024; heat provided free by AWS via Heatworks utility
expansion_details: Exploring geothermal and additional renewable sources for diversification
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Tallaght District Heating Network officially opened. AWS supplies 100% of heat. 1,500 tonnes CO2 savings per annum projected.

facility_name: Equinix HE3/HE5 Helsinki -- World's First DC District Heating (Since 2010, with Helen)
build_phase_if_known: World's first DC heating project since 2010 (Kanavaranta). Expanded to HE3 (Suvilahti) and HE5 (Viikinmaki). Partnership with Helen extended multiple times, most recently Oct 2024.
cooling_type: Water-based cooling with heat pump recovery; waste heat raised by Helen heat pumps and transferred to 1,409km district heating network
opportunity_summary: Pioneered the world's first data center district heating project in 2010 at Kanavaranta, Helsinki. Partnership with Helen (Helsinki's energy company) expanded to HE3 (Suvilahti) and HE5 (Viikinmaki). Heat pumps raise DC waste heat temperature for transfer into Helen's 1,409km district heating network. Heats up to ~1,500 one-bedroom apartments per site. Extended again in Oct 2024 for even more homes. Over a decade of continuous operation.
confidence_reason: Confirmed by Equinix newsroom press releases, Helen (Helsinki energy) official announcements, DCD news coverage, Bloomberg video feature. Partnership history since 2010 documented by multiple independent sources.
source_1_title: Equinix and Helen extend successful cooperation to distribute more waste heat from data centers
source_1_evidence_snippet: Equinix and Helen pioneered the first data center heating project in the world at Kanavaranta, heating homes in Helsinki since 2010.
source_2_title: More Helsinki homes to be heated using excess heat from Equinix data centers
source_2_evidence_snippet: Excess heat from Equinix data center can meet the annual heating demand of up to almost 1,500 one-bedroom apartments.
notes: HEAT RECOVERY. World's first DC district heating (2010). Helen's network is 1,409km long, 90% of Helsinki buildings connected. Multiple expansions over 14+ years. HE3 Suvilahti and HE5 Viikinmaki now active. ~1,500 apartments per expansion. Liquid cooling CONFIRMED by water-based heat pump system.
heat_recovery_details: World's first DC heat recovery (2010); Helen heat pumps; 1,409km district network; ~1,500 apartments per site; multiple expansions; over 14 years of operation
current_phase: Expanded and operational across three Helsinki sites
expansion_details: Oct 2024 extension announced for more Helsinki homes
pipeline_evidence_snippet: More Helsinki homes to be heated using excess heat from Equinix data centers. Partnership extended Oct 2024.

facility_name: Equinix PA10 Paris Saint-Denis -- Olympic Aquatic Centre + 1,000 Homes Heated
build_phase_if_known: Opened 2023. 5,000+ sqm colocation. ~2,250 cabinets. Heat recovery to Plaine Saulnier and Olympic Aquatic Centre. SMIREC operates heat network.
cooling_type: Water-based cooling with heat exchanger and heat pump; 6.6MW heat capture capacity; surplus heat transferred to SMIREC district heating network
opportunity_summary: Equinix PA10 was designed and built specifically for heat recovery. Captures 6.6MW of waste heat, equivalent to heating 1,000+ homes plus the Paris 2024 Olympic Aquatic Centre swimming pools. Heat provided free of charge for 15 years to Plaine Saulnier urban development zone. SMIREC operates the heat network. Approximately 10,000 MWh/year. Also features a 430sqm rooftop greenhouse growing tomatoes, strawberries, and eggplants.
confidence_reason: Equinix official resources, Data Center Knowledge, The B1M video documentary, Data Centre Magazine, and Financial World all confirm. Olympic swimming pool heating widely covered during Paris 2024.
source_1_title: Paris 2024: Excess Data Center Heat Used to Warm Olympic Swimming Pools
source_1_evidence_snippet: Heat network capturing 6.6MW from PA10. Equivalent to heating 1,000 homes plus the Olympic swimming pool annually.
source_2_title: Waste heat powers a greenhouse on PA10 Paris data center
source_2_evidence_snippet: PA10 opened in 2023 designed and built to recover and export heat. Rooftop greenhouse 430sqm.
notes: HEAT RECOVERY. Purpose-built for heat export. 6.6MW capture capacity. ~10,000 MWh/year. Heated Paris 2024 Olympic swimming pools at 27-28C. Free heat for 15 years to Plaine Saulnier. SMIREC heat network operator. 430sqm rooftop greenhouse. Liquid cooling CONFIRMED by heat exchanger water system.
heat_recovery_details: 6.6MW heat capture; ~10,000 MWh/year; 1,000+ homes + Olympic Aquatic Centre; free heat for 15 years; SMIREC network operator; 430sqm rooftop greenhouse
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Equinix PA10 captures 6.6MW of waste heat for district heating including Olympic Aquatic Centre.

facility_name: Equinix FR4/FR6/FR8 Frankfurt -- Waste Heat to 1,000 Households (Vonovia/EB-SIM)
build_phase_if_known: Three sites (FR4, FR6, FR8) connected to heat exchanger. Heat network built with Vonovia, AS Enterprise Engineering, EB-SIM. Operational from 2025.
cooling_type: Water-based cooling with heat exchanger connected to district heating network; three DC sites feeding single heat exchange system
opportunity_summary: Equinix partners with housing company Vonovia, engineering firm AS Enterprise Engineering, and investment advisor EB-SIM to build a heating network in north Frankfurt Griesheim. Three data center sites (FR4, FR6, FR8) connected to heat exchanger. Supplies ~1,000 households cost-neutrally from 2025. Avoids local fossil fuel combustion. Part of Germany's new EnEfG requirements (10% waste heat utilization mandatory from July 2026).
confidence_reason: Equinix press release, DCD news report, Data Centre Review, Clean Energy Wire all confirm. Partnership with Vonovia and EB-SIM documented. Germany's EnEfG regulatory context verified.

source_1_evidence_snippet: From 2025, around 1,000 households to be supplied cost-neutrally with excess heat from Equinix FR4, FR6, and FR8.
source_2_title: Equinix joins district heating scheme in Frankfurt, Germany
source_2_evidence_snippet: Partnership with Vonovia, AS Enterprise Engineering, and EB-SIM to build heating network in north Frankfurt Griesheim.
notes: HEAT RECOVERY. Three Equinix sites feeding single heat network. Vonovia housing + EB-SIM investment. ~1,000 households from 2025. Germany EnEfG mandates 10% waste heat use from July 2026 for new DCs (15% in 2027, 20% in 2028). Liquid cooling CONFIRMED by heat exchanger.
heat_recovery_details: Three sites (FR4/FR6/FR8) to heat exchanger; ~1,000 households cost-neutral; Vonovia/EB-SIM partners; operational from 2025
pipeline_evidence_snippet: From 2025, around 1,000 households to be supplied cost-neutrally with excess heat from local Equinix data centers.

facility_name: Equinix TR5 Markham -- Waste Heat to 14M sqft Mixed-Use Development (MDE)
build_phase_if_known: MDE heat pump project commissioned late 2023. Supplies heat to residential buildings, hotel, university, shopping center in downtown Markham.
cooling_type: Water-based cooling with heat export to Markham District Energy (MDE) heat pump system
opportunity_summary: Heat exported from Equinix TR5 is distributed via Markham District Energy (MDE, est. 2000) to over 14 million sqft of mixed-use development including residential buildings, a hotel, university, and local shopping center in downtown Markham. MDE heat pump project commissioned late 2023. MDE was established by City of Markham after 1998 ice storm. IBM Canada was first anchor customer. Excess heat must be rejected in summer when demand is low.
confidence_reason: Confirmed by Equinix blog post, Data Center Knowledge, Interesting Engineering case study, and Equinix Heat Export press release. MDE partnership documented by multiple sources.
source_1_title: The Hidden Benefit of Data Centers: Warming Communities, Not Just Servers
source_1_evidence_snippet: Heat exported from TR5 distributed throughout multiple residential buildings, hotel, university and shopping center in downtown Markham via MDE heat network.
source_2_title: How to Put the Heat from Data Centers to Good Use
source_2_evidence_snippet: MDE heat pump project for heat recovery commissioned beginning in late 2023. Over 14 million sqft of mixed-use development served.
notes: HEAT RECOVERY. Canadian DC heat recovery. MDE established 2000 by City of Markham after 1998 ice storm. IBM first anchor customer. 14M sqft mixed-use served. Excess heat rejected in summer. Liquid cooling CONFIRMED by heat pump water export.

pipeline_evidence_snippet: Heat exported from TR5, distributed to multiple residential buildings, hotel, university in downtown Markham.

facility_name: STACK Infrastructure OSL01A Oslo -- 3.5MW Heat Export to 5,000 Homes (Hafslund Oslo Celsio)
build_phase_if_known: Heat recovery concept conceived 2018. Heat-exchange plant built on campus. Fully operational September 2022 after one-year ramp-up.
cooling_type: Retrofitted cooling coils and insulated pipework; waste heat transferred to Hafslund Oslo Celsio 60-mile thermal energy distribution system via dedicated heat-exchange plant on campus
opportunity_summary: STACK's OSL01A data center near the heart of Oslo exports 3.5MW of heat energy into Oslo's district heating system, representing ~25GWh of energy per year. Heats up to 5,000 homes. Partnership with Hafslund Oslo Celsio, which operates a 60-mile thermal distribution system. Concept conceived 2018, tested and proven, dedicated heat-exchange plant built on campus with retrofitted insulated pipework and cooling coils. Fully operational Sept 2022. Frees up Celsio's green electricity for transport electrification.
confidence_reason: STACK official press release, Cision distribution, DCD, Data Centre Review, Yahoo Finance, Capacity Media all confirm. 3.5MW / 25GWh / 5,000 homes figures consistent across all sources.

pipeline_evidence_snippet: STACK Infrastructure connects data center to Hafslund Oslo Celsio system. 3.5MW / 25GWh / 5,000 homes.

Won 2018 Energy Awards Renewable Energy Technology of the Year in London.

facility_name: White Data Center Bibai -- Snow-Cooled DC with Eel Farm + Greenhouse (Hokkaido, Japan)
build_phase_if_known: Operational. Snow-cooled. Eel farming since Jan 2024 (1700 elvers). First shipment July 2024. Greenhouse for mushrooms, coffee, abalones, sea urchins.
cooling_type: Snow meltwater cooling system (snow stored in insulated mound); waste heat at 33C used for eel farming tanks and greenhouse agriculture
opportunity_summary: White Data Center in Bibai, Hokkaido uses a unique snow cooling system: snow collected in winter is stored in an insulated mound. Server heat slowly melts the snow, and the cold meltwater cools antifreeze pipes. After cooling the DC, water exits at 33C -- ideal for Japanese eel farming. 1,700 elvers imported Jan 2024, first Yuki Unagi (snow eel) shipment July 2024. Adjacent greenhouse grows mushrooms, coffee beans, abalones, sea urchins, and spinach using waste heat. World-unique circular model.
confidence_reason: CNN, DCD, TechSpot, Tom's Hardware, Japanese government publication (Highlighting Japan), and White Data Center official announcements all confirm. First shipment July 2024 documented.

facility_name: Telehouse / Enwave Toronto (151 + 250 Front St) -- Deep Lake Water Cooling + Heat Recovery
full_address: 151 and 250 Front Street West, Toronto, Ontario, Canada
build_phase_if_known: Operational. Enwave Deep Lake Water Cooling System from Lake Ontario (4C intake). Waste heat converted to green heat for city water heating. Enwave Green Heat Plant at Pearl Street.
cooling_type: Enwave Deep Lake Water Cooling (DLWC) system -- 4C water from Lake Ontario via three intake pipes; waste heat from DCs upgraded by electrified heat pump into district heating hot water
opportunity_summary: Telehouse Canada operates DCs at 151 and 250 Front Street West in Toronto, cooled by Enwave's Deep Lake Water Cooling System (DLWC). Three intake pipes draw 4C water from Lake Ontario to cool ~190 buildings including data centers, hospitals, and campuses. Waste heat from DCs is leveraged by Enwave's Green Heat Plant (Pearl Street) -- an electrified heat pump upgrades waste heat into district heating hot water. Saves Telehouse water, electricity, and infrastructure costs. Part of North America's largest district cooling system.

build_phase_if_known: Acquired from KPN late 2023. 6,225 sqm. Being developed to 8MW IT capacity. Ready H1 2026. 100% residual heat to Polderwarmte for Waarderpolder business park.
cooling_type: Water-based cooling with 100% residual heat recovery to Polderwarmte district heating system for Waarderpolder business park (1,100 businesses)
opportunity_summary: Penta Infra's AMS01 in Haarlem (acquired from KPN) is being developed to 8MW IT capacity. Letter of intent with Polderwarmte to redirect 100% of residual heat into district heating system for Waarderpolder business park (1,100 businesses). Ready H1 2026. Municipality of Haarlem supports the project toward gas-free Waarderpolder by 2040.

facility_name: Leafcloud Distributed Leaf Sites -- Amsterdam Cloud Servers Heating Apartments, Pools, Nursing Homes
build_phase_if_known: Operational. Distributed 'Leaf sites' inside apartment buildings, swimming pools, nursing homes. Captures 95%+ heat. Joined NVIDIA AI startup programme.
cooling_type: Advanced liquid cooling capturing 95%+ of server heat; direct heat transfer to building heating systems (domestic hot water, pool heating, ATES systems)
opportunity_summary: Leafcloud operates distributed micro data centers ('Leaf sites') installed inside existing buildings across Amsterdam. Each site uses advanced liquid cooling capturing 95%+ of server heat for direct building use. Targets: swimming pools, nursing homes, apartment buildings, hospitals, office complexes, ATES systems. Reduces CO2 by up to 1,776 kg per kW-year. Reduces energy costs by up to 40% for public pools. Joined NVIDIA AI startup programme. Cloud services: Kubernetes, GPU, OpenStack.
confidence_reason: Leafcloud official site, Corporate Knights, Silicon Canals, and NVIDIA partnership all confirm. 95%+ heat capture and distributed model documented.
source_1_title: Leaf Sites: Sustainable Data Centers That Heat Buildings
source_1_evidence_snippet: Distributed data centers inside buildings. Liquid cooling captures 95%+ heat. Swimming pools, nursing homes, apartments.
source_2_title: Companies are finding creative ways to use heat from data centres
